title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men have been charged with kidnapping and assaulting a 28-year-old man near Winter Park.
Police report suspects beat and tied the victim with an extension cord, causing facial injuries and bleeding.
Arrests were made following a video evidence; suspects released on bail, arraignment scheduled Monday.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila were arrested at Logan Airport by ICE while trying to board flights home.
Rosales, protected under DACA, and Ardila, an asylum seeker, face detention due to immigration status issues.
Their attorney, Todd Pomerleau, has filed legal challenges to keep them in Massachusetts during immigration proceedings.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Kevin Slane suggests movies like 'Interstellar' and 'Lawrence of Arabia' to watch after 'The Odyssey.'
'O Brother, Where Art Thou?' and 'The Searchers' offer thematic parallels to Nolan's 'The Odyssey.'
'The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie' humorously adapts elements of Homer's classic epic in an aquatic setting.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their win streak to 11 games, the franchise's longest since 2016, after a doubleheader sweep of the Tampa Bay Rays on Friday.
In Game 1, Boston defeated the Rays 10-0, with pitcher Jake Bennett allowing only one hit in six innings.
Game 2 ended in a 5-3 victory for the Red Sox, who now hold the American League's third wild-card spot, improving to a .500 record.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez could return to the Red Sox rotation as soon as July 21 after a left groin strain.
Connelly Early is progressing well from left elbow inflammation but has no set return date yet.
Kutter Crawford, Tanner Houck, and Johan Oviedo are making strides in their rehabilitation processes.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and contributed three hits to lead Boston in Game 1's victory over the Rays.
Caleb Durbin went 5 for 7 across the doubleheader, boosting his season performance as a consistent hitter.
Jake Bennett pitched six scoreless innings in Game 1, allowing only one hit, easing the Red Sox bullpen use.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots owner Robert Kraft voiced support for coach Mike Vrabel amid controversy over alleged affair with journalist Dianna Russini.
Vrabel, who led Patriots to 2025 Super Bowl, denied affair claims and missed part of the NFL draft for personal reasons.
Dianna Russini resigned from The Athletic following an internal investigation into her interaction with Vrabel at an Arizona resort.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Anya Taylor-Joy stars in "Lucky," a crime series on Apple TV, featuring a family entangled in theft and deception.
"Obsession," a 2026 horror film that became a box office hit, is now streaming on Peacock.
"The Hunt for the Wilderpeople," starring the late Sam Neill, is available on Prime Video and Plex.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin, 25, violated NH Civil Rights Act by assaulting a transgender woman in a Concord convenience store in May 2024.
The court ordered Lufkin to avoid contact with the victim and imposed a $5,000 civil fine, with $4,000 suspended.
Lufkin pleaded guilty to assault charges and received a 12-month sentence, with some time suspended, as part of the plea deal.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox's trade deadline plans remain 'fluid' as the Aug. 3 deadline approaches, according to ESPN's Jeff Passan.
Despite winning 10 consecutive games, Boston is cautious due to past season mediocrity and potential future performance.
Key trade discussions involve Aroldis Chapman and the need for a right-handed hitter, per ESPN and insider Jon Morosi.
